items.title: Honor’s Robot phone is real, and coming later this year
items.summary:
The camera nods, dances to music, and tracks faces. The rest of the specs are a secret. Something unusual happened at Mobile World Congress this year. A device sat in a glass case at the Honor booth in Hall 3,  present, demonstrably functional, performing little robotic gestures for anyone who stopped to watch,  and yet […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
items.summary: While Washington’s breakup with Anthropic exposed the complete lack of any coherent rules governing artificial intelligence, a bipartisan coalition of thinkers has assembled something the government has so far declined to produce: a framework for what responsible AI development should actually look …

items.title: International Women’s Day: Women Hold Just 18% Leadership Roles In India But Outpace Men In AI Readiness
items.summary: On International Women's Day, new data from LinkedIn highlights that women remain underrepresented in India’s workforce, with women making up 35.5% of entry-level roles but only 28.1% of the overall workforce. The data also shows that women with bachelor’s degrees experience the steepest drop in …

items.title: OpenAI robotics chief quits over AI’s potential use for war and surveillance
items.summary: A top robotics executive at OpenAI said Saturday she had resigned over the company’s deal with the US Department of Defense to allow its artificial intelligence to be used for war and potential domestic surveillance.
